rulings.Case Title: REGINA PACHECO VS. MINDFUL VENTURE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Court'S Ruling On Defendants' Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Ari Qayumi
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Tuesday, December 10, 2024, Line 1. DEFENDANTS ARI QAYUMI, AND MINDFUL VENTURE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, LLC's Motion For Reconsideration Of Court's Ruling On Defendants' Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment. The "motion for reconsideration of court's ruling on defendants' motion to set aside default judgment" is denied. A reconsideration motion "must be based upon new or different facts, circumstances or law" and a valid reason for not presenting them earlier. (CCP 1008; Gilberd v. AC Transit (1995) 32 Cal.App.4th 1494, 1500.) The moving party must state by affidavit "what new or different facts circumstances, or law are claimed to be shown." (CCP 1008.) This defendants do not do. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: KRISTIAN RICARDO FIGUEROA LOPEZ ET AL VS. ARCHITECTURAL SURFACES GROUP, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Lx Hausys America Inc. To Produce Its Custodian Of Records For Deposition And Produce Documents At Deposition And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Tuesday, December 10, 2025, Line 2. PLAINTIFF's Motion To Compel Defendant Lx Hausys America Inc. To Produce Its Custodian Of Records For Deposition And Produce Documents At Deposition And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Plaintiff's motion to compel Defendant Lx Hausys America Inc. to produce its custodian of records and produce documents is granted. Discovery is liberally granted in California. (CCP 2017.010 ["reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence"].) While plaintiff's 123 categories of documents are broad, defendant fails to present competent evidence showing an undue burden. (CCP 2017.020(a) [limits on discovery].) The motion is therefore granted. Plaintiff to recover $1,000 as reasonable sanctions. The deposition shall commence by January 10, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL HENRY VS. ERIC YOUNG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Tuesday, December 10, 2024, Line 6. CROSS DEFENDANT PAUL HENRY's MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. Paul Henry's motion for judgment on the pleadings as to Eric Young's first amended cross-complaint for declaratory and injunctive relief (FACC) is denied. Young pleads that he owns intellectual property obtained from Cerego Japan as his employment compensation and that Henry seeks assignment of this same IP to help satisfy a judgment he has against a former employer, Cerego, Inc. (FACC 1:27-2:8, 4:27-5:4.) Thus, this is a classic scenario for CCP 1060 declaratory relief. Covering territory that was (or should have been) covered by an earlier unsuccessful demurrer, Henry's motion makes two unavailing arguments. First, he says he and Young must have a "relationship." (Memo. 6:4-5.) They do: both want the same IP. But CCP 1060 is not so restricted in any event. Second, Henry says the FACC seeks an "advisory opinion." (Memo. 6:12.) But what it actually seeks is not conjectural: "a judicial determination of the respective ownership, rights and duties" of Young and Henry "with respect to the intellectual property rights." (FACC 5:5-10.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: BREDA MITCHELL VS. MARTINA MITCHELL ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Tuesday, December 10, 2024, Line 3. 2 - DEFENDANT MARTINA MITCHELL's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT. Ordered off calendar. On November 19, the court ordered plaintiff to comply with Local Rule 2.7(B) by providing courtesy copies of her belated oppositions to defendant's pending demurrer and motion to strike. The court has since received two courtesy copies of plaintiff's opposition to the motion to strike, but still no copy of the demurrer opposition. Upon plaintiff's complete compliance with Local Rule 2.7(B), the demurrer and motion to strike may be re-set for a date after December 24. Papers shall bear the new hearing date. =(302/RBU)
